title: 10 best herbal tinctures for upset stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tannins present in yellow gentian work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, yellow gentian's bitter compounds stimulate digestion, improving the movement of food through the digestive system and reducing discomfort. As a result, herbal yellow gentian tinctures offer effective relief from upset stomachs and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Bitter principles: Yellow Gentian's bitter compounds stimulate digestion, improve appetite, and relieve digestive discomfort by increasing saliva production and gut motility.
Antispasmodic properties: The tincture's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and pain associated with upset stomach.
Anti-inflammatory activity: Yellow Gentian's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe and protect the digestive lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant are used to make medicinal tinctures as they contain bitter compounds that help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes, which are underground stems, have a high concentration of alkaloids that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing upset stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried gentiana lutea roots by cutting into smaller pieces and storing in airtight containers for later use.
Chop the dried gentiana lutea roots into small fragments to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds.
Combine 10 parts chopped gentiana lutea roots with 40 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, then seal tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to promote extraction and infusion of bioactives into the tincture.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the finished tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The flavonoids and glycyrrhizin present in licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. This, in turn, relieves symptoms such as heartburn, acid reflux, and nausea. Additionally, licorice tinctures can stimulate digestion and absorption of nutrients, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and alleviating digestive discomfort. By taking herbal licorice tinctures, individuals may experience rapid relief from stomach upset and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root that has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to calm digestive irritation and reduce oxidative stress in the gut.

Saponins: The saponin content in licorice root has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing acid production and inflammation, while also promoting the healing of ulcers and other gastrointestinal lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it effective in calming upset stomachs.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and saponins, the barks of licorice plant help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots at dawn when their medicinal properties are most potent and store them in an airtight container.
Clean the harvested roots by gently scrubbing them with water and mild soap to remove dirt and debris before rinsing thoroughly.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:4 parts root to solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate extraction, allowing the glycyrrhizin to dissolve into the solvent.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with nausea and indigestion. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help stimulate digestion and relieve cramps, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger tincture stimulates digestive enzymes, such as gastric amylase, lipase, and trypsin, which helps to break down proteins, carbohydrates, and fats, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger contains a potent anti-inflammatory compound called gingerol, which reduces inflammation in the digestive tract and relieves pain and discomfort associated with stomach upset, such as cramps and bloating.

Musculotropic Agent: Ginger's musculotropic properties help to relax and reduce spasms in the smooth muscle of the digestive tract, which can calm symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), diarrhea, and stomach cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm the digestive system.
Roots: Ginger roots have been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, indigestion, and bloating, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots weighing 200g and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Peel the ginger roots using a vegetable peeler removing any papery skin layers.
Cut the peeled ginger into small pieces and combine them with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to infuse the tincture.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel from the aloe plant contains mucilages, which are natural emollients that can help calm digestive irritation and reduce inflammation in the gut. When taken as a tincture, these compounds are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream and travel to the stomach where they can provide relief from symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and cramps. This can help restore balance to the digestive system and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), constipation, and other gastrointestinal issues.

Soil-antibacterial properties: The sulfated glycoproteins in aloe vera have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as E. coli, Salmonella, and Shigella, which can cause gut infections and stomach upset, while also promoting a healthy gut microbiome.

Gastroprotective effects: Aloe vera's mucilages and polysaccharides help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, reducing irritation and damage caused by stomach acid and digestive enzymes, which can alleviate symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated stomachs, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
Leaves: Aloe leaves are rich in anthraquinones, which have natural laxative properties that can help alleviate constipation and relieve stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves, washing them thoroughly with distilled water and drying with a clean towel.
Slice the aloe leaves into thin strips, measuring 100 grams for every liter of tincture you wish to make.
Combine sliced aloe with 70% ethyl alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensuring the liquid covers the aloe completely, then seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar at room temperature away from direct sunlight, allowing it to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. This can provide relief from symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, fenugreek has been shown to stimulate digestion and increase the production of gastric enzymes, which helps to break down food more efficiently and alleviate discomfort. Overall, herbal fenugreek tinctures can offer a natural and effective solution for managing upset stomach issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Fenugreek contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as bloating and abdominal pain.
Gastroprotective properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in fenugreek tincture may help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing the risk of gastritis and ulcers.
Antacid properties: Fenugreek contains mucilage, a type of carbohydrate that forms a protective barrier on the stomach lining, helping to neutralize stomach acid and soothe heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of fenugreek have been traditionally used to soothe and calm digestive issues, making them a popular choice for creating medicinal tinctures to alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to coat and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried fenugreek seeds trigonella foenum graecum from trusted sources.
Grind 10 grams of dried fenugreek seeds into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered fenugreek with 50 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature shaking daily before straining.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth into dark glass bottle and store in cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm digestive issues. The flavonoids present in chamomile have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the muscles help relax the stomach, reducing spasms and allowing for easier digestion. As a result, herbal chamomile tinctures provide fast and effective relief from upset stomachs, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Sooothing and relaxing properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help ease spasms and cramps in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.

Gastroprotective effects: Chamomile's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by gastric acid, which can reduce the risk of gastritis, ulcers, and other stomach disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are used in medicinal tinctures as they contain a higher concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant are used to make tinctures for upset stomach due to their soothing effect on digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers during full bloom in warm sunny weather.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator or air drying method for 7 days.
Combine 250 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka and store for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with upset stomach because its calming properties help to soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried.

Fill the jar with 80-proof vodka, covering the herbs completely, and store in a cool dark place for 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley tinctures helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and soothe the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tinctures helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried carum carvi seeds and measure out 100 grams for each batch.

Mix 250 milliliters of high-proof vodka with the powdered caraway seeds in a clean glass jar, sealing tightly after.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, allowing it to steep and mature.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried burdock root arctium lappa approximately one cup.
Measure one part dried burdock root with two parts vodka or grain alcohol.
Combine dried burdock root and solvent in a clean glass jar tightly sealed.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place allowing it to macerate for six weeks shaking weekly.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

Fill the jar with 80 proof vodka leaving about an inch of space at the top and secure it tightly to allow for fermentation and extraction over 4-6 weeks.
Store the jar in a cool dark place like a pantry or cupboard, shaking it gently every day or two to facilitate the extraction process taking approximately 60 days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and labeling the bottle with the date of preparation for future use.

Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to five parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily, for optimal solvent penetration.

Clean the harvested material by gently washing it with filtered water and pat drying with paper towels.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for upset stomach are concentrated plant extracts that can provide quick relief from digestive discomfort. These tinctures work by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms, and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria. Examples include peppermint, ginger, and chamomile tinctures, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm nausea and alleviate bloating. For individuals with chronic stomach issues, these tinctures can greatly improve their quality of life by allowing them to enjoy meals without discomfort and reducing the need for antacids or other medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat upset stomach with herbal tinctures is to combine Ginger root tincture for its anti-inflammatory properties, Peppermint leaf tincture to soothe digestive issues, and Dandelion root tincture to calm stomach cramps. Mix 10-15 drops of each tincture in water and drink three times a day as needed. This blend helps to ease nausea, reduce bloating, and promote digestion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, nausea and vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, and bloating. Herbal remedies like peppermint, ginger, chamomile, and licorice root tinctures can help soothe the digestive system, calm inflammation, and ease symptoms associated with these conditions. These natural treatments are often gentle on the stomach and may be more effective than over-the-counter medications in some cases.
